title: Building a holodeck at Sony Pictures with Marble and World Labs | Brent Liang posted on the topic | LinkedIn
text:
Brent Liang
6mo
Edited
Report this post
This is how we built a real-life holodeck at Sony Pictures.

All scenes powered by Marble, the
World Labs
multimodal world model that generates high-fi, persistent 3D worlds from just a single image.

Grateful to partner on this project with the incredible team, led by Fei-Fei Li, the modern godmother of AI. 

Watch the behind-the-scenes of "Just Imagine" (1.5M views and counting). Presented by the World Labs team,
Henry Williams
,
Richard Zheng
and
Ben S.
from a16z New Media.

#media
#launch

Transcript
World Labs believes that spatial intelligence is the linchpin technology for creativity, for experiences, for simulation and embodied AI. Spatial intelligence is about machines ability to understand how objects and events are positioned in 3D space and time. And we really view this long arc of the company as building and realizing the dreams of spatial intelligence. You as a human look at the world in front of you and you just have such an innate understanding of all of the objects, all their relationships and a material properties, all of these kind of deep contextual information. No one has really built a model that truly captures that yet. What if we could create an entire world where you can explore every angle and you can look around every corner? To put it simply, marvelous for building worlds. People will be able to take a tech simpa image input, video input to build out a 3D space. As we lunge barbell, we realize we need some kind of video to show the world what marble is and how to use it. Oh wow. Oh, oh, while they're two things. Welcome to Hollywood. Thank you. How does it feel for you guys seeing the technology you've been building in real life? Oversight. It's big, larger than life. We've only been looking at this on desktops, and this is awesome. The goal of the new media team at age 16 Z is to tell stories about the companies that we invest in. When we found out that we were going to help them with the launch, it was like, OK, how do we not only tell a really great story, but also showcase exactly what the product is? That's where our concept of this virtual production kind of came into play. It really allows you to see what is capable with the technology. This is the first virtual set production ever where this background right here is completely AI generated with marble. They pitched to us this idea of working with the virtual production using marble sink and we look at the calendar. This is what, a four weeks away? No one has ever done this before. It's such a crazy idea. But we like crazy ideas, so we're like, let's do it. When they came to us with this project and the turn around was quite fast, it was very exciting to us. Shooting on an LED wall basically means you can kind of go to any location you can think of. Currently we're in front of the Colosseum and even we have the control to adjust the size. We can spin it, we can change the direction of the light. All of those things would be extremely time consuming and expensive to do practically. We start with getting to creative, using AI generation to figure out what the shots generally would look like. I take that and transfer it to Unreal Engine and I start blocking out what angles we need to be at what height. Then we bring in the Gaussian slats and see where we need to move it and scale it to get that angle because the spots have this inherent photorealistic quality. So it actually took off a lot of the payload. This is 40X faster than the traditional workflow. How many things did we make? I think about 300 in the process of working through this. That would be impossible in a matter of weeks. This could not have been done traditionally. It's a different order of magnitude speed. Virtual production moves so fast and so many decent go wrong so quick. What Marvel world labs and 1st reduction? I think there's a really cool synergy by having just a quick turnaround of iteration and troubleshooting. And our tools are great, but they take so long which limits how the imagination can flow and and here I feel like I'm just inside someone's imagination. It really unlocks the possibilities for the creators so they don't have to worry about if I have this idea, how much time do I have to build it? This is a world that we can travel through, and the fact that we can do this with AI is incredible. This film is that journey of a creative person, which is all of us, who goes from her childhood imagination all the way to adulthood. That progression is very beautiful of seeing someone grow into their interests and like doing very meaningful creative work. We were really able to like, capture that emotion of what it's like to be a kid and have these imaginary worlds come to life, literally. I think anybody that watches this hopefully will be like, wow, I want to be creative, I wanna tell cool stories. I wanna be so imaginative again that anything could be possible.
